title: OpenAPI 3.2, Compatibility, Deployment, Draining, and Recovery · Contract Lens: Production Defense Recovery
summary: Documentation differs from runtime, examples contain secrets, additive changes break strict clients, storage contracts before old revisions drain, and rollback cannot restore invariants. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts production defense recovery increment in versioned API contract and production release defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Defend outcomes, toolchain, semantics, Node and Express behavior, validation, limits, persistence, identity, policy, browser and proxy trust, tests, telemetry, capacity, compatibility, release, rollback, data recovery, and residual risk.
Build once, verify provenance and configuration, expand storage, stage readiness, canary, observe, stop admission, drain HTTP and background work, then contract safely.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Production Defense Recovery · Theory Production Defense Recovery
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 3206 in a burst intake service near its event-loop and queue limits. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate one contract-compatible, expand-first, canary, drain, and recoverable release; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then make OpenAPI and runtime disagree on nullability and block the release. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend outcomes, toolchain, semantics, Node and Express behavior, validation, limits, persistence, identity, policy, browser and proxy trust, tests, telemetry, capacity, compatibility, release, rollback, data recovery, and residual risk.
